findings.effect: Splice site variant, no functional enzyme
findings.risk_summary: Reduced DPD enzyme activity causes slow clearance of fluoropyrimidines, leading to severe, potentially fatal toxicity (severe neutropenia, mucositis, diarrhea) at standard doses.
Reduced NUDT15 activity causes toxic accumulation of active thiopurine metabolites; especially important in East and South Asian populations where this variant is more common than TPMT deficiency.

findings.effect: Missense, decreased function. Common in East/South Asian populations, often missed by Western-focused panels.
findings.risk_summary: Reduced UGT1A1 enzyme activity causes toxic accumulation of the active irinotecan metabolite (SN-38), leading to severe neutropenia and diarrhea at standard doses.

disclaimer: This tool is a research/screening aid, not a diagnostic device. All flagged results must be confirmed by a certified clinical pharmacogenomics lab and interpreted by a qualified oncologist/clinical pharmacist before any dosing decision.
